topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp, cream-colored paper felt like sandpaper under my fingertips as I unfolded the document. Every carefully chosen anecdote, every carefully phrased achievement, was a stranger's voice. My name was there, of course, but the narrative unfolding beside it belonged entirely to someone else. A cold, tight knot began to constrict in my chest, squeezing the air from my lungs. I reread the section detailing my early struggles, the descriptions of my 'defining moments,' and a bitter taste rose in my mouth. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the hardwood floor. My hands were trembling, and I needed to get away from the desk, away from the evidence of this profound betrayal.

[story 2]

It started as a casual glance, a misplaced footnote in an old yearbook. Then, the realization hit me like a physical blow. The timeline they presented—the career trajectory, the personal milestones—was a meticulously crafted fiction, a portrait painted by an unseen hand. I stared at the words, my jaw tightening until my teeth ached. A flush crept up my neck, spreading across my cheeks. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to crumple the paper into a tight ball and throw it across the room, but my limbs felt strangely heavy, rooted to the spot. I just stood there, breathing shallowly, the silence in the room amplifying the frantic thumping in my ears.

[story 3]

When the curator handed me the bound volume, the weight of it felt monumental, a physical manifestation of the deception. I flipped through the pages, searching for any misplaced detail, any flicker of truth that contradicted the narrative presented. Nothing. It was seamless, polished, and utterly false. A deep, hollow ache settled in my core, making my stomach clench. I looked up at the person who had presented it, my expression carefully neutral, but my eyes betrayed a frantic internal storm. I managed a thin, strained smile, the muscles in my face refusing to cooperate, as I swallowed hard, the sound unnaturally loud in the quiet gallery space.
